model_narrative: We are studying a network of chemical reactions. Some chemicals are available from the start as raw materials or catalysts, and others are not available at all. Each reaction can produce one chemical by consuming a group of other chemicals. We decide for every chemical whether it can be present in the system or not. The objective is to make the presence indicator of one particular target chemical as small as possible, which tells us whether that target chemical can be produced at all.

sets.name: v
sets.doc: all chemicals in the system, including raw materials, catalysts, intermediates, and the final target chemical

sets.name: rx
sets.doc: the chemical reactions, each of which can produce one chemical from a group of other chemicals

sets.name: yavail
sets.doc: the chemicals that are available on hand from the start, namely the raw materials and catalysts we already possess; each of these is forced to be present

sets.name: ynotavail
sets.doc: the chemicals that are not available and cannot be obtained; each of these is forced to be absent

sets.name: rxv
sets.doc: the pairs of a reaction and the chemical that this reaction produces; for each such pair the reaction has a known group of input chemicals that it consumes

params.name: logicc
params.doc: a zero or one flag that is one when the chemical in the third position is one of the input chemicals consumed by the reaction in the first position to make the chemical in the second position, and zero otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: 1 if the chemical can be present or produced in the system, 0 if it is absent. Available chemicals are fixed to 1 and unavailable chemicals are fixed to 0

vars.name: totsum
vars.doc: presence indicator of the target chemical, used as the objective value; it equals 1 when the target chemical can be produced and 0 otherwise

constraints.name: y[vv]
constraints.intent: For each reaction together with a chemical it can produce, the reaction can only make that chemical when all of the input chemicals it consumes are present. State this as a requirement that the produced chemical may be present only if every one of the inputs that the reaction uses to make it is also present.

constraints.name: totsum
constraints.intent: Tie the objective value to whether the target chemical can be produced. Set the objective value equal to the presence indicator of the target chemical.

query: Procurement is floating a leaner sourcing plan: across the three reagents y10, y12 and y22 they'd carry at most two of them on hand at any time rather than all three. Plug that ceiling in and tell me where producibility for acetone lands.
expected_pyomo:
model.y['y10'].unfix()
model.y['y12'].unfix()
model.y['y22'].unfix()
model.cap_reagents = Constraint(expr=model.y['y10'] + model.y['y12'] + model.y['y22'] <= 2)

query: There's a proposal on the table to gate reagent y22 behind the same approval as compound y16, which we currently can't source at all. In effect y22 could only be stocked if y16 is also available. Run it and let me see whether acetone stays producible.

query: We're looking at a storage-safety rule that won't let catalysts y25 and y26 sit in inventory together — at most one of the two can be on hand. What does the optimizer return for acetone producibility under that restriction?

query: Engineering wants to trial a discontinuation sweep that drops both materials y31 and y33 from the active inventory in one move. Apply that across the pair and walk me through whether acetone can still be made.
expected_pyomo:
model.y['y31'].unfix()
model.y['y33'].unfix()
def _drop_rule(model, v):
    return model.y[v] <= 0 if v in ['y31', 'y33'] else Constraint.Skip
model.drop_y31_y33 = Constraint(model.v, rule=_drop_rule)

query: The board is pitching a minimal-footprint scenario over the trio y10, y22 and y31: keep at most one of those three on hand at a time. Plug that in and tell me what the optimizer says about producing acetone.

model.y['y22'].unfix()

query: We're carrying all four catalysts y25, y26, y31 and y33 in the plan, which feels heavier than this process should need. Shouldn't we be able to run with at most three of those four stocked at once? What's keeping the model from making that move?
expected_pyomo:
model.y['y25'].unfix()
model.y['y26'].unfix()
model.y['y31'].unfix()
model.y['y33'].unfix()
model.cap_catalysts = Constraint(expr=model.y['y25'] + model.y['y26'] + model.y['y31'] + model.y['y33'] <= 3)

query: Material y31 keeps getting stocked even though it shares handling requirements with compound y19, which we never have available. Couldn't we just hold y31 to the same standard — only on hand when y19 is — and still make acetone? What's the model seeing that I'm not?

query: The plan leans on the whole slate of stocked materials y22, y25, y26, y31 and y33 at full strength. Wouldn't it be obvious to trim at least one and run with no more than four of those five on hand? What's the tradeoff that's pulling it the other way?
expected_pyomo:
model.y['y22'].unfix()
model.y['y25'].unfix()
model.y['y26'].unfix()
model.y['y31'].unfix()
model.y['y33'].unfix()
model.atmost4_five = Constraint(expr=model.y['y22'] + model.y['y25'] + model.y['y26'] + model.y['y31'] + model.y['y33'] <= 4)

model.drop_y10_y12 = Constraint(model.v, rule=_drop_rule)

query: Material y33 only ever shows up alongside catalyst y26, so carrying it independently looks redundant. Shouldn't we be able to stock y33 only when y26 is already on hand and still make acetone? What's keeping the model from making that move?
